questions.question: Explain the primary purpose of the JLabel component in a Swing-based GUI and describe two common ways to set its text content.
keyPoints: JLabel is a non-editable component used to display short text strings, an image, or both.
Text can be set during construction using `new JLabel("Text")`
Text can be set after construction using the `setText(String text)` method.

questions.question: Describe the process of creating and displaying an image on a JLabel using ImageIcon. Include code snippets for the key steps: loading the image and applying it to the label.
keyPoints: Create an ImageIcon object by providing the path to the image file, e.g., `ImageIcon icon = new ImageIcon("path/to/image.png");`
The ImageIcon handles loading the image from the specified file.
Set the icon on the JLabel using the `setIcon(Icon icon)` method, e.g., `myLabel.setIcon(icon);`
Alternatively, pass the ImageIcon directly to the JLabel constructor: `JLabel label = new JLabel(icon);`

questions.question: A developer needs a JLabel that displays both an icon and text, with the text positioned to the right of the icon. Explain how to configure the JLabel's horizontal alignment and text position properties to achieve this layout. Additionally, discuss how the component's behavior changes when the containing frame is resized and which layout managers would be most and least suitable for controlling the label's position.
keyPoints: Use `setHorizontalTextPosition(SwingConstants.RIGHT)` to position the text to the right of the icon.
Use `setHorizontalAlignment(SwingConstants.LEFT)` to keep the entire label (icon + text) aligned to the left within its container area.
Upon resizing, a JLabel's size is determined by its layout manager. A FlowLayout will allow it to remain at its preferred size, while a BorderLayout will stretch it to fill the assigned region.
BorderLayout (CENTER) is least suitable as it stretches the label. FlowLayout or GridBagLayout are more suitable for maintaining the label's preferred size and precise component placement.
